summary: F4 appears closed in the inspected code, but F5 still allows stale frames from a replaced socket because liveness is checked only by pairingId, not by the specific WebSocket that delivered the frame.

findings.invariant: credential-socket binding: frames from a revoked/replaced socket cannot resolve a delegation or emit a ledger row post-revoke
findings.title: Replaced socket frames still pass the liveness guard for the same pairingId
findings.recommendation: Pass the delivering WebSocket into onMessage and require getLiveConnection(pairingId)?.ws === ws before any side effect, or add a registry helper such as isLiveConnection(pairingId, ws). The current isLive(pairingId) guard stays true after a redial because the replacement socket keeps the pairingId live, so a late message from the retired socket can still resolve delegation_result, denial, or ledger_row frames.
